contract.summary: SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory is seeking firm fixed price proposals for Mirror Benders, with proposals due by May 29, 2026.
contract.why_it_matters: This is a direct RFP for scientific equipment (laboratory equipment and supplies, analytical laboratory instrument manufacturing) that Thomas Scientific could potentially supply or facilitate.

contract.summary: SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory is inviting firm fixed price proposals for Photon Stopper Holders, with a submission deadline of June 4, 2026.
contract.why_it_matters: This is a direct RFP for scientific equipment (laboratory equipment and supplies, analytical laboratory instrument manufacturing) that Thomas Scientific could potentially supply or facilitate.

project.summary: Fermilab has completed and delivered the final cryomodules for the high-energy upgrade of SLAC's Linac Coherent Light Source (LCLS), which will double the energy of the beam and more than double the maximum X-ray energy. The installation and commissioning process is expected to take about two years.
project.scope: Doubling the energy of the LCLS X-ray laser beam and more than doubling the maximum X-ray energy by adding 23 cryomodules to the superconducting accelerator.
project.timeline: Decade-long production run completed; installation and commissioning started December 2025 and expected to take about two years.
project.why_it_matters: The ongoing installation and commissioning of the LCLS high-energy upgrade will drive demand for specialized scientific equipment, maintenance supplies, and potentially new reagents for experiments utilizing the enhanced X-ray capabilities.

project.summary: SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory and Citrine Informatics have formed a two-year public-private partnership, funded by the Energy Department's Advanced Manufacturing Office (AMO), to develop a methodology for discovering new materials using AI and SLAC's Stanford Synchrotron Radiation Lightsource (SSRL).
project.scope: Developing a methodology for discovering new materials applicable across manufacturing, initially focusing on metallic glasses and optimizing nanoparticle catalyst synthesis, utilizing AI and SSRL.
project.why_it_matters: This new research program will likely require advanced computational resources, specialized materials for experimentation, and analytical consumables for the Stanford Synchrotron Radiation Lightsource (SSRL).

project.summary: SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory is leading the construction of the massive camera for the Large Synoptic Survey Telescope (LSST), which will conduct a 10-year survey of the night sky, cataloging billions of stars and galaxies and generating vast amounts of data.
project.project_name: Large Synoptic Survey Telescope (LSST) / Vera C. Rubin Observatory Camera Construction
project.scope: Construction of the largest digital camera for optical astronomy and its use in a 10-year survey to study dark energy, dark matter, and galaxy formation.
project.why_it_matters: The ongoing construction and future operation of the LSST camera will create demand for high-precision optical components, specialized detectors, data storage solutions, and various laboratory consumables for calibration and maintenance.

ai_summary: SLAC is undergoing significant expansion and innovation, with major upgrades to LCLS, new AI-driven materials discovery initiatives, and leading the LSST camera construction. These long-term projects, alongside active procurement for specialized components like Mirror Benders and Photon Stopper Holders, signal increased demand for a wide range of lab supplies, consumables, and specialized equipment over the next several years.
